rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HOAI VO TRAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens Pursuant Cal. Civ. Pro §405.31 Or In The Alternative Cal Code Civ Pro. §405.32; Request Of Court Order That Petitioner Post An Undertaking Pursuant Cal. Code Civ Pro. §405.38; Notice Of Hearing Thereon And Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Respondents' Motion to Expunge Lis Pendens is granted. Petitioner fails to assert the requisite real property claim pursuant to Code of Civ. Proc. Section 405.31. Petitioner alleges that Respondents stole tangible personal property from the estate, which does not qualify as a real property claim under Code of Civ. Proc. Section 405.4. Petitioner's proposed equitable remedy of a constructive trust is also an insufficient basis for a lis pendens. (Lewis v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 1850, 1862.) The Court grants $1,500 in attorney's fees associated with filing the motion. The prevailing party shall prepare a proposed order. Any party choosing to contest the tentative ruling must notify the Court and opposing counsel no later than July 22, 2015, at 4:00 p.m.
